title: At launch: capture margin/stop-out + final swap specs; verify forced-liquidation (§14) envelope clearance
source: Organizer clarification 2026-06-17: margin/stop-out details + final swap/financing specs published "when trading access opens" (~launch Jun 21 22:00 BST).
why: The §14 forced-liquidation red-line = IMMEDIATE ELIMINATION. Our sleeve hard-latch (0.045 portfolio-kill) and survival caps are sized against ASSUMED margin/stop-out (margin=notional/30). Once the org publishes real margin + stop-out at launch, re-verify the lottery sleeve envelope AND survival-book caps cannot trigger a margin call under worst-case path. Also confirmed: no commissions; equity carries over between rounds ($1M initial) — both already match the B0183 sim.
recommendation: At launch pull live margin/stop-out from MT5 symbol specs + org docs; re-run the sleeve worst-case envelope check against real stop-out; tighten portfolio-kill only if real margin is tighter than notional/30.

history.reason: PM 2026-06-19 pre-computed the launch-day section14 reference (docs/submission/b0195_section14_launch_memo_2026-06-19.md) by calling the REAL instrument functions (pipeline/stage2_lambda: margin_call_move/lam_max_safe/section14_safe_lambda, exact-parity to simulate_barbell). Key result: R1 lambda=1.5 breaches only at -65.5% (M=30) / -64.9% (M=20) -> ~+37pt cushion vs the worst-real single-round crypto move -27.6%, and stays SAFE (deploy unchanged) even if the real maintenance divisor is much tighter than 30:1; lambda<=2.0 safe at every M tested; only lambda=2.4 scales down (to 2.341 at M=30/w=-0.40/g=0.5, or 2.100 at w=-0.45). This turns B0195 at the flip into a CONFIRM-the-banner step (pull real maintenance/stop-out M -> --stage2-margin-div M -> match Table 3) rather than a derive-under-pressure step; the instrument is fail-closed (scales down/refuses) against any tighter-than-expected M. B0195 stays proposed (launch-day execution needs the real spec at the flip); memo left UNCOMMITTED-adjacent (new doc, committable) but deferred to the deliberate launch-doc commit. Decision rule unchanged: lambda<=2.0 at w=-0.40, or lambda>2.0 only with --stage2-worst-case-move -0.45.

LAUNCH-DAY §14 re-verify 2026-06-21 with TODAY'S live data (CEO-requested). Read-only MT5 margin probe (acct 10251 / FTWorldwide / $1M / 1:30, trade_mode=2): mt5.order_calc_margin implied maintenance divisor M = 30.000 BTC / 29.998 ETH / 30.004 SOL -> real M~=30 matches the assumed 30:1 EXACTLY; account margin_so_call=50%, margin_so_so(stop-out)=0.0% -> real forced-liq fires near 0% margin level, MORE lenient than the model's 100% trigger (model conservative). Worst single-round crypto move 2023-2026 (validate_barbell, today's data): 24h -24.9% / 48h -27.6% (unchanged benchmark). At λ=1.5, M=30: §14 breach boundary c*(λ)=-65.5% -> +37.9pt cushion vs -27.6% worst-real; M-insensitive. Arm-time banner (rehearsed) confirms 'λ=1.500 within the combined-safe ceiling -> deploying unscaled (no §14 haircut)'; combined ceiling (book gross 0.50 folded)=2.341. CONCLUSION: the frozen command's DEFAULT --stage2-margin-div 30 is now VERIFIED correct; no override needed; NO forced-liquidation path at λ=1.5 (combined crypto -27.6% + book -10% -> margin level 11.4). The anti-liquidation limit is SAFE. NOTE: arming is separately BLOCKED by B0202 (a kill-switch accounting bug, not a §14 risk). B0195 stays proposed: re-confirm the banner against live M at the actual 21:00Z flip.
